item.title: UK to invest $66 million in critical minerals to reduce import reliance
item.description: Britain will invest £50-million ($66-million) to boost domestic production of critical minerals, the government said on Monday, as it seeks to reduce reliance on concentrated global supply chains and strengthen economic resilience. The funding will support projects across extraction, processing and recycling, aimed at securing materials used in products ranging from smartphones and fridges to electric vehicle batteries. (via Australasia Global Mining News) #SouthAfrica #SouthAfricaNews
